title: Model-workstation type
description: Configure type = MODEL_WORKSTATION for model-oriented prompts, output parsing, and stop words through a MODEL_WORKER backend.
openingSummary: MODEL_WORKSTATION is the WorkstationType for model-oriented prompt files, outcome parsing, output schemas, and stop words. Set type to MODEL_WORKSTATION when a step must run through a MODEL_WORKER with those prompt/parse surfaces, and choose behavior independently for STANDARD, REPEATER, CRON, or POLLER scheduling. Keep provider-neutral operation bindings on MODEL_INVOKE instead.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: Model-workstation is the Factory WorkstationType for model-oriented prompt and parse surfaces in you-agent-factory. This page covers the type = MODEL_WORKSTATION discriminator, how MODEL_WORKSTATION pairs with a MODEL_WORKER backend and exclusive promptFile, outcomeFormat, outputSchema, and stopWords fields, the validated type overlay embed, a minimal valid configuration, an incompatible misuse that borrows model-invoke operation bindings, operational cautions, and compatible WorkstationKind behaviors. Use it when a workstation must author model prompts and parse outcomes rather than bind provider-neutral operations, run an agent loop, or dispatch inference, script, or poller workers.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: The discriminator field is type with value MODEL_WORKSTATION. MODEL_WORKSTATION selects the runtime that dispatches a named MODEL_WORKER for prompt-file and outcome-parse work and selects the exclusive promptFile, outcomeFormat, outputSchema, and stopWords fields. Type is independent of behavior: MODEL_WORKSTATION pairs with every published WorkstationKind, including STANDARD and CRON. MODEL_WORKSTATION is not a scheduling behavior and is not MODEL_INVOKE — keep operation and operationBindings on the model-invoke type. Both share MODEL_WORKER companions, but their selected field sets stay separate.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Author a workstation with type MODEL_WORKSTATION and bind a MODEL_WORKER. Choose behavior for the scheduling axis you need — STANDARD for fire-once dispatch, REPEATER for change-triggered loops, CRON for schedules, or POLLER for long-lived poller ingress. Wire inputs plus outputs, onContinue, onRejection, and onFailure so model outcomes route correctly. Set promptFile for the authored prompt, and use outcomeFormat, outputSchema, or stopWords when this step needs structured parse or stop control. Do not set operation, operationBindings, openCodeAgent, classificationRoutes, or cron fields that belong to other WorkstationType or WorkstationKind families. Choose MODEL_INVOKE for provider-neutral operation bindings, AGENT_RUN for agent loops, or INFERENCE_RUN for bounded inference harness dispatch.

variantFields.title: Variant Fields
variantFields.body: The embed below shows the live Workstation base definition with MODEL_WORKSTATION overlay applicability. MODEL_WORKSTATION selects the exclusive promptFile, outcomeFormat, outputSchema, and stopWords fields. Excluded fields belong to other WorkstationType families or to CRON scheduling and must not appear on this type. Open the full Factory schema reference for exhaustive field lookup rather than copying contract text into this page.

examples.title: Examples
examples.body: Use the minimal valid example when starting a MODEL_WORKSTATION with behavior STANDARD, a promptFile, and outcome parse fields. The misuse example borrows operation from MODEL_INVOKE — that field is excluded from MODEL_WORKSTATION.

operationalCautions.title: Operational Cautions
operationalCautions.body: MODEL_WORKSTATION steps fail closed when the bound worker is missing or is not a MODEL_WORKER, when concurrency or resource limits block dispatch, when route fields leave an outcome unmapped, or when authors place model-invoke, agent-run, classifier, or cron exclusives on this type. Do not use operation under MODEL_WORKSTATION — that field belongs on MODEL_INVOKE. Keep scheduling on behavior and runtime on type.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is the direct /docs/workstations/model-workstation surface for MODEL_WORKSTATION runtime. It is not a sync of packaged CLI docs, not the MODEL_INVOKE type guide, and not the STANDARD, REPEATER, CRON, or POLLER behavior contracts. Compatible WorkstationKind companions name the scheduling pairing for this type; they do not redefine MODEL_WORKSTATION fields. Worker backends stay on the workers family.

links.companionIntro: MODEL_WORKSTATION requires a MODEL_WORKER companion and is compatible with every published WorkstationKind. Bind the model worker, then choose scheduling on the behavior axis. MODEL_INVOKE also uses MODEL_WORKER but selects operation fields instead of prompt/parse surfaces.
links.misuseNote: operation belongs on MODEL_INVOKE. Keep MODEL_WORKSTATION on identity, worker binding, inputs, outputs, routing fields, and promptFile / outcomeFormat / outputSchema / stopWords only.
links.failureWorkerMissingCause: No worker bound, or bound worker is not a MODEL_WORKER — author and bind a model backend
links.failureWorkerMismatchCause: Bound worker is AGENT_WORKER, INFERENCE_WORKER, or another non-model type — switch to MODEL_WORKER or change workstation type
links.failureConcurrencyCause: Workstation or resource limits blocked model dispatch — raise limits or reduce parallel work
links.failureRoutingCause: Outcome has no authored route — wire outputs, onContinue, onRejection, or onFailure
links.failureOperationMisuseCause: operation declared under MODEL_WORKSTATION — move provider-neutral operation bindings to type MODEL_INVOKE
